commands.name: uname
commands.description: Print system information about the operating system and hardware

patterns.text: show all system information at once
patterns.explanation: Displays kernel name, hostname, version, architecture, and OS

patterns.text: find out what kernel version is running
patterns.explanation: Prints only the kernel release version

patterns.text: check the system hardware architecture
patterns.explanation: Shows the machine hardware name like x86_64 or arm64

patterns.text: find out the operating system name
patterns.explanation: Prints the kernel name such as Linux or Darwin

commands.name: hostname
commands.description: Show or set the system hostname

patterns.text: display the current hostname of the machine
patterns.explanation: Prints the system hostname

patterns.text: show the fully qualified domain name
patterns.explanation: Displays the FQDN of the machine

patterns.text: get all IP addresses associated with the hostname
patterns.explanation: Lists all network addresses assigned to the host

patterns.text: temporarily change the system hostname
patterns.explanation: Sets the hostname until the next reboot

commands.name: uptime
commands.description: Show how long the system has been running and load averages

patterns.text: check how long the server has been running
patterns.explanation: Shows time since boot, number of users, and load averages

patterns.text: show uptime in a human-readable pretty format
patterns.explanation: Displays uptime in a friendly format like 'up 3 days, 5 hours'

patterns.text: find out when the system was last booted
patterns.explanation: Shows the exact date and time when the system started

patterns.text: check the current system load averages
patterns.explanation: The 1, 5, and 15 minute load averages are shown at the end of output

commands.name: date
commands.description: Display or set the system date and time

patterns.text: show the current date and time
patterns.explanation: Displays the current date, time, and timezone

patterns.text: display the date in ISO 8601 format
patterns.explanation: Shows date and time in ISO format like 2024-01-15T10:30:00+00:00

patterns.text: get the current Unix timestamp
patterns.explanation: Outputs seconds since the Unix epoch (January 1, 1970)

patterns.text: format the date as year-month-day
patterns.explanation: Displays the date in a custom format like 2024-01-15 14:30:00

patterns.text: convert a Unix timestamp to a human-readable date
patterns.explanation: Converts the given epoch timestamp to a readable date

commands.name: cal
commands.description: Display a calendar in the terminal

patterns.text: show this month's calendar
patterns.explanation: Displays the current month's calendar with today highlighted

patterns.text: show the calendar for an entire year
patterns.explanation: Displays all 12 months of the specified year

patterns.text: display a specific month and year
patterns.explanation: Shows the calendar for March 2025

patterns.text: show the previous, current, and next month together
patterns.explanation: Displays three months side by side centered on the current month

commands.name: timedatectl
commands.description: Control the system time, date, and timezone settings

patterns.text: show the current time and timezone configuration
patterns.explanation: Displays local time, UTC time, timezone, and NTP sync status

patterns.text: change the system timezone
patterns.explanation: Sets the system timezone to Eastern Time

patterns.text: enable automatic time synchronization via NTP
patterns.explanation: Turns on Network Time Protocol for automatic clock sync

patterns.text: list all available timezones
patterns.explanation: Shows every timezone that can be set on the system

patterns.text: manually set the system date and time
patterns.explanation: Sets the system clock to the specified date and time

commands.name: systemctl
commands.description: Control the systemd system and service manager

patterns.text: check the status of a service
patterns.explanation: Shows whether nginx is running, its PID, and recent log entries

patterns.text: start a stopped service
patterns.explanation: Starts the nginx service immediately

patterns.text: enable a service to start automatically at boot
patterns.explanation: Creates symlinks so the service starts on boot

patterns.text: restart a service to apply configuration changes
patterns.explanation: Stops and then starts the service

patterns.text: list all running services
patterns.explanation: Shows all services currently in the running state

patterns.text: check if a service is enabled at boot
patterns.explanation: Returns whether the service is set to start at boot time

commands.name: service
commands.description: Run a System V init script to manage services (legacy)

patterns.text: start a service using the legacy service command
patterns.explanation: Starts the Apache web server

patterns.text: check the status of a service
patterns.explanation: Shows whether the MySQL service is running

patterns.text: restart a service to reload its configuration
patterns.explanation: Restarts the networking service

patterns.text: list all available services and their statuses
patterns.explanation: Shows all init scripts with their running/stopped status

commands.name: journalctl
commands.description: Query and display logs from the systemd journal

patterns.text: view recent system logs
patterns.explanation: Shows the last 50 log entries from the journal

patterns.text: follow logs in real time like tail -f
patterns.explanation: Continuously displays new log entries as they appear

patterns.text: view logs for a specific service
patterns.explanation: Shows all log entries from the nginx service

patterns.text: show logs from the current boot only
patterns.explanation: Filters logs to only show entries since the last boot

patterns.text: view logs within a specific time range
patterns.explanation: Shows log entries between the specified start and end times

patterns.text: show only error and critical log messages
patterns.explanation: Filters log output to error priority and above

commands.name: dmesg
commands.description: Display kernel ring buffer messages

patterns.text: view kernel messages with human-readable timestamps
patterns.explanation: Shows kernel log messages with readable date and time

patterns.text: watch kernel messages in real time
patterns.explanation: Continuously displays new kernel messages as they occur

patterns.text: show only error and warning messages from the kernel
patterns.explanation: Filters kernel messages to show only errors and warnings

patterns.text: check for hardware detection messages after plugging in a USB device
patterns.explanation: Shows the most recent kernel messages where USB detection appears

patterns.text: clear the kernel ring buffer
patterns.explanation: Clears all messages from the kernel ring buffer

commands.name: sysctl
commands.description: Read and modify kernel parameters at runtime

patterns.text: show all kernel parameters
patterns.explanation: Lists all available kernel parameters and their current values

patterns.text: check if IP forwarding is enabled
patterns.explanation: Displays the current value of the IP forwarding parameter

patterns.text: enable IP forwarding for routing traffic between interfaces
patterns.explanation: Temporarily enables IPv4 packet forwarding

patterns.text: reload sysctl settings from the configuration file
patterns.explanation: Loads settings from /etc/sysctl.conf to make changes persistent

patterns.text: increase the maximum number of open file handles system-wide
patterns.explanation: Sets the kernel limit for the total number of open files

commands.name: lscpu
commands.description: Display information about the CPU architecture

patterns.text: show detailed CPU information
patterns.explanation: Displays CPU architecture, cores, threads, model, caches, and features

patterns.text: find out how many CPU cores are available
patterns.explanation: Filters output to show the total number of CPU cores

patterns.text: check if the CPU supports virtualization
patterns.explanation: Shows the virtualization technology supported by the CPU

patterns.text: display CPU info in a parseable format
patterns.explanation: Outputs CPU information in JSON format for scripting

commands.name: lsblk
commands.description: List information about block devices (disks and partitions)

patterns.text: show all disks and their partitions
patterns.explanation: Lists all block devices in a tree format showing disks and partitions

patterns.text: show disk sizes and filesystem types
patterns.explanation: Displays filesystem type, label, UUID, and mount point for each device

patterns.text: list only physical disks without partitions
patterns.explanation: Shows only top-level disk devices without their partitions

patterns.text: show disk sizes in human-readable bytes
patterns.explanation: Displays device sizes in bytes for precise measurements

patterns.text: display specific columns like name, size, and type
patterns.explanation: Shows only the selected columns in the output

commands.name: lspci
commands.description: List all PCI devices in the system

patterns.text: show all PCI hardware devices
patterns.explanation: Lists all PCI buses and devices connected to them

patterns.text: show detailed information about all PCI devices
patterns.explanation: Displays verbose details including drivers and memory regions

patterns.text: find the graphics card model
patterns.explanation: Filters PCI devices to show only the VGA-compatible graphics controller

patterns.text: show PCI device tree structure
patterns.explanation: Displays PCI devices in a tree hierarchy showing bus connections

commands.name: lsusb
commands.description: List USB devices connected to the system

patterns.text: list all connected USB devices
patterns.explanation: Shows all USB buses and connected devices with vendor and product IDs

patterns.text: show detailed information about all USB devices
patterns.explanation: Displays verbose device descriptors for all USB devices

patterns.text: display USB devices in a tree hierarchy
patterns.explanation: Shows USB device topology as a tree with speed and driver info

patterns.text: find a specific USB device by vendor ID
patterns.explanation: Filters to show only devices from the specified vendor (e.g., Logitech)

commands.name: lshw
commands.description: List detailed hardware configuration of the machine

patterns.text: show a complete hardware inventory of the system
patterns.explanation: Displays full hardware details including CPU, memory, storage, and network

patterns.text: show hardware summary in a short format
patterns.explanation: Provides a concise overview of all hardware components

patterns.text: display only memory and RAM information
patterns.explanation: Filters output to show memory modules, sizes, and speed

patterns.text: show network hardware details
patterns.explanation: Lists network interface hardware including drivers and capabilities

patterns.text: output hardware info as HTML for a report
patterns.explanation: Generates an HTML formatted hardware report

commands.name: dmidecode
commands.description: Read hardware information from the system BIOS/DMI table

patterns.text: show all BIOS and hardware information from DMI tables
patterns.explanation: Dumps the full DMI/SMBIOS table with BIOS, system, and hardware data

patterns.text: show RAM module details like size, speed, and type
patterns.explanation: Displays information about installed memory modules

patterns.text: find the system manufacturer and model
patterns.explanation: Shows the system manufacturer, product name, serial number, and UUID

patterns.text: check the BIOS version and release date
patterns.explanation: Displays BIOS vendor, version, and release date information

patterns.text: find the maximum supported RAM capacity
patterns.explanation: Shows physical memory array info including maximum capacity

commands.name: free
commands.description: Display amount of free and used memory in the system

patterns.text: check how much RAM is available on the system
patterns.explanation: Shows total, used, free, and available memory in human-readable units

patterns.text: display memory usage in megabytes
patterns.explanation: Shows memory and swap usage in megabytes

patterns.text: show memory totals only
patterns.explanation: Adds a total row combining RAM and swap

patterns.text: continuously monitor memory usage every 2 seconds
patterns.explanation: Refreshes the memory display at 2-second intervals

commands.name: vmstat
commands.description: Report virtual memory, CPU, and IO statistics

patterns.text: show a snapshot of system performance statistics
patterns.explanation: Displays process, memory, swap, IO, and CPU statistics

patterns.text: monitor system performance every second for 10 iterations
patterns.explanation: Samples and displays stats every second for 10 rounds

patterns.text: show disk IO statistics
patterns.explanation: Displays read and write statistics for each disk device

patterns.text: display memory stats in megabytes for readability
patterns.explanation: Shows memory values in megabytes instead of kilobytes

patterns.text: show active and inactive memory
patterns.explanation: Displays active and inactive memory instead of buffer and cache

commands.name: iostat
commands.description: Report CPU and I/O statistics for devices and partitions

patterns.text: show CPU and disk IO statistics
patterns.explanation: Displays average CPU utilization and device IO statistics since boot

patterns.text: monitor disk IO in real time every 2 seconds
patterns.explanation: Shows extended disk statistics updating every 2 seconds

patterns.text: show only disk statistics in megabytes
patterns.explanation: Displays disk throughput in megabytes per second

patterns.text: monitor a specific disk device
patterns.explanation: Shows extended stats for sda every second for 5 samples

patterns.text: show IO stats in human-readable format with timestamps
patterns.explanation: Displays human-readable IO stats with timestamps every 2 seconds

commands.name: sar
commands.description: Collect, report, and save system activity information

patterns.text: show CPU usage statistics for the current day
patterns.explanation: Displays CPU utilization data collected throughout the day

patterns.text: monitor memory usage over time
patterns.explanation: Reports memory utilization every second for 10 intervals

patterns.text: check disk IO activity history
patterns.explanation: Shows block device activity statistics from collected data

patterns.text: view network interface throughput statistics
patterns.explanation: Shows network device statistics every second for 5 samples

patterns.text: show load averages and run queue length
patterns.explanation: Displays run queue length and load averages at 1-second intervals

commands.name: who
commands.description: Show who is currently logged into the system

patterns.text: see who is currently logged in
patterns.explanation: Lists all currently logged-in users with their terminals and login times

patterns.text: show logged-in users with idle time and PID
patterns.explanation: Displays users with idle time, PID, and login information

patterns.text: count the number of logged-in users
patterns.explanation: Shows usernames and a count of all logged-in users

patterns.text: show the current user and terminal info
patterns.explanation: Displays information about the current session only

commands.name: w
commands.description: Show who is logged in and what they are doing

patterns.text: see who is logged in and what commands they are running
patterns.explanation: Shows logged-in users, idle time, and their current processes

patterns.text: check what a specific user is doing on the system
patterns.explanation: Filters output to show only the specified user's sessions

patterns.text: show logged-in users without the header line
patterns.explanation: Displays user activity without the uptime header

patterns.text: show short output with just user and their process
patterns.explanation: Shows a condensed view without login time and CPU times

commands.name: last
commands.description: Show listing of last logged-in users

patterns.text: show recent login history
patterns.explanation: Lists the most recent user logins and system reboots

patterns.text: check when the system was last rebooted
patterns.explanation: Shows a history of system reboots with timestamps

patterns.text: show login history for a specific user
patterns.explanation: Filters login records to show only the specified user

patterns.text: show logins from the past 7 days
patterns.explanation: Shows login records within the last week

patterns.text: display IP addresses for remote logins
patterns.explanation: Shows the IP address column for each login entry

commands.name: lastb
commands.description: Show listing of failed login attempts

patterns.text: check for failed login attempts to detect intrusions
patterns.explanation: Lists all failed login attempts from the btmp log

patterns.text: show failed login attempts for a specific user
patterns.explanation: Filters failed attempts to show only the specified user

patterns.text: show failed logins with IP addresses
patterns.explanation: Displays the source IP address for each failed login

patterns.text: show the most recent failed logins limited to 20 entries
patterns.explanation: Shows only the 20 most recent failed login attempts

commands.name: id
commands.description: Display user and group identity information

patterns.text: show my user ID, group ID, and group memberships
patterns.explanation: Displays UID, GID, and all groups for the current user

patterns.text: check the user and group info for another user
patterns.explanation: Shows UID, GID, and groups for the specified user

patterns.text: get just the numeric user ID
patterns.explanation: Outputs only the effective user ID number

patterns.text: list all group IDs the current user belongs to
patterns.explanation: Shows all supplementary group IDs for the current user

commands.name: groups
commands.description: Display group memberships for a user

patterns.text: see what groups the current user belongs to
patterns.explanation: Lists all groups the current user is a member of

patterns.text: check group memberships for another user
patterns.explanation: Shows all groups the specified user belongs to

patterns.text: verify if a user is in the sudo group
patterns.explanation: Checks whether the user has sudo group membership

patterns.text: list groups for multiple users at once
patterns.explanation: Shows group memberships for each specified user

commands.name: env
commands.description: Display or set environment variables and run commands in modified environments

patterns.text: list all environment variables
patterns.explanation: Prints all current environment variables and their values

patterns.text: run a command with a temporary environment variable
patterns.explanation: Sets DEBUG=true only for the duration of the command

patterns.text: run a command with a clean environment
patterns.explanation: Starts a shell with all environment variables cleared

patterns.text: run a program with a modified PATH
patterns.explanation: Prepends a directory to PATH for the executed command only

commands.name: printenv
commands.description: Print all or specific environment variables

patterns.text: show the value of a specific environment variable
patterns.explanation: Prints the value of the HOME variable

patterns.text: display all environment variables sorted alphabetically
patterns.explanation: Lists all environment variables in sorted order

patterns.text: check the current PATH directories
patterns.explanation: Shows the directories in the PATH variable

patterns.text: check if a specific environment variable is set
patterns.explanation: Tests whether the variable exists and has a value

commands.name: set
commands.description: Set or unset shell options and positional parameters

patterns.text: show all shell variables and functions
patterns.explanation: Displays all shell variables, functions, and their values

patterns.text: enable strict error handling in a bash script
patterns.explanation: Exits on error (-e), undefined variables (-u), and pipe failures

patterns.text: enable debug mode to trace script execution
patterns.explanation: Prints each command and its arguments before executing

patterns.text: disable filename globbing in the shell
patterns.explanation: Prevents wildcard characters from expanding to filenames

commands.name: export
commands.description: Set environment variables available to child processes

patterns.text: set a new environment variable that persists for child processes
patterns.explanation: Creates an environment variable accessible to spawned processes

patterns.text: add a directory to the system PATH
patterns.explanation: Appends a new directory to the existing PATH

patterns.text: list all exported environment variables
patterns.explanation: Displays all variables marked for export with their values

patterns.text: set the default text editor
patterns.explanation: Sets vim as the default editor for programs that respect EDITOR

patterns.text: remove a variable from the environment
patterns.explanation: Unexports the variable so it is no longer passed to child processes

commands.name: ulimit
commands.description: Get and set user resource limits for the shell and its children

patterns.text: show all current resource limits
patterns.explanation: Displays all soft limits including open files, stack size, and CPU time

patterns.text: check the maximum number of open file descriptors
patterns.explanation: Shows the current limit on open files per process

patterns.text: increase the open file limit for the current session
patterns.explanation: Sets the maximum open files to 65536 for this shell session

patterns.text: check the maximum stack size
patterns.explanation: Shows the maximum stack size in kilobytes

patterns.text: show hard limits that cannot be increased by regular users
patterns.explanation: Displays all hard resource limits set by the system

commands.name: locale
commands.description: Get locale-specific information about language and regional settings

patterns.text: show all current locale settings
patterns.explanation: Displays the active locale settings for language, numbers, and dates

patterns.text: list all available locales on the system
patterns.explanation: Shows every locale that is installed and available

patterns.text: check the current character encoding
patterns.explanation: Displays the character map name like UTF-8

patterns.text: display all locale categories with their source
patterns.explanation: Shows all keywords and values for the time locale category

commands.name: hostnamectl
commands.description: Query and change the system hostname and related settings

patterns.text: show the current hostname and system info
patterns.explanation: Displays static/transient hostname, OS, kernel, and architecture

patterns.text: permanently change the system hostname
patterns.explanation: Sets the static hostname that persists across reboots

patterns.text: set a pretty descriptive hostname for display purposes
patterns.explanation: Sets a human-friendly hostname with spaces and special characters

patterns.text: check the operating system and kernel version
patterns.explanation: Filters output to show OS name and kernel version

patterns.text: view the machine chassis type
patterns.explanation: Shows whether the system is a desktop, laptop, server, or VM
